titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/47

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=323 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:36:46.446055+00 | translation moa=318 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:36:44.982131+00

div.p:
. W . Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue . francis et Anglicis . et Scottis  salutem . Sciant tam po
steri quam presentes me concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse ecclesie Sancte Marie de Maylros et monachis
ibidem deo seruientibus donationem quam Walterus filius Alani fecit eidem ecclesie et monachis de terra Ed
mundestun . in omnibus ita libere et quiete in elemosina perpetua  sicut liberius et quietius carta ipsius Walteri eis testatur .
Teste Ric’ . episcopo Sancti Andree . Engelramo Episcopo Glasguensi . Iohanne Abbate de kelchou . Osberto abbate de
Gedewrde . Nicholao Cancellario . Ric’ . Capellano . Ric’ . de Morauilla . Waltero filio alani . Philippo de
Valunnes . Roberto Auenel . Waltero Corbet . Gilleb’ filio Richerii . Willelmo de haia . Willelmo de Morte
mer . Radulfo namo . Imero Clerico . Waltero de berchelai . Apud lannarc .

p.cl: William king of the Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, French and English and Scots, greeting.

Let those to come as well as those present know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose and the monks in that place serving God the donation that Walter son of Alan made, to the same church and to the monks, in respect of the land of 'Edmundestoun'; in all things so freely and quitely in everlasting alms, as freely and quitely the charter of Walter himself to them bears witness.

Witness Richard, Bishop of St Andrews; Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; John, abbot of Kelso; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Nicholas the chancellor; Richard the chaplain; Richard de Morville; Walter son of Alan; Philip de Valognes; Robert Avenel; Walter Corbet; Gilbert son of Richer; William de la Haye; William de Mortimer; Ralph le Nain; Imer the clerk; Walter of Berkeley. At Lanark.
